hypotheses.statement: [Falsifiable claim — e.g., 'Security operations managers at mid-market companies spend >4 hours/week manually correlating alerts across multiple tools']
hypotheses.test: [Specific test — e.g., 'Ask 10 SOC managers to walk through their last alert triage. Count how many mention manual correlation and time spent.']
hypotheses.falsification_criteria: [REQUIRED — What would disprove this — e.g., 'Fewer than 3 of 10 describe manual correlation, or average time <1 hour/week']

hypotheses.statement: [Second hypothesis — e.g., 'Mid-market security teams would pay $25K-50K/year for automated alert correlation']
hypotheses.test: [Specific test — e.g., 'Run Van Westendorp in 5+ interviews. Check if optimal price falls within range.']
hypotheses.falsification_criteria: [REQUIRED — What would disprove this — e.g., 'Van Westendorp optimal price <$15K or >$75K, or buyers say budget caps at $20K']

schema.category: purpose | problem | market | wedge | competition | defensibility | pricing | buying_process
schema.priority: P1 (existential — must be true) | P2 (important) | P3 (nice to validate)
schema.test: The specific action that would confirm or falsify this
schema.falsification_criteria: REQUIRED — What result would disprove this hypothesis. Not optional in v2.
schema.created_cycle: Forge number when hypothesis was created
schema.last_updated_cycle: Forge number of last status/confidence change
